entries.title: Deploy protocol
entries.description: PM instructs DeployM. DeployM commits + pushes. GitHub Pages auto-deploys from main. Never force-push.

entries.title: Verification batch commit pattern
entries.description: Verification batches follow a structured commit message format: feat(verify): Batch N — M pass, K fail, summary line. Body includes: skill type, pass/override breakdown, SM/SecM/MemM evolve cycle counts, and final collection state (X pass / Y fail / Z unverified / W% coverage). Multiple commits per session are normal — one per batch plus one for catalog/infrastructure changes.
examples: 70ab8adc: feat(catalog): dual catalog alignment — extract installs field, unified priority scoring
c7b2e22f: feat(verify): Batch 11 — 100 agent skills verified, SM --type filter, separate tier display

entries.title: Post-verification rebuild is mandatory before deploy
entries.description: After any verification run, PM instructs WS3 to rebuild before DeployM commits. Required order: (1) run_verify_strict_5agent.py, (2) build_json + build_html + build_indexes, (3) DeployM commits all modified data/skills/*.json + site/api/* + data/stats.json together. Skipping rebuild means site shows stale data. This was a workflow gap identified 2026-03-01.
